1. The root directory structure (list all top-level files and folders)

1	#!/usr/bin/env node
2	import { add } from "@/src/commands/add"
3	import { build } from "@/src/commands/build"
4	import { diff } from "@/src/commands/diff"
5	import { docs } from "@/src/commands/docs"
6	import { info } from "@/src/commands/info"
7	import { init } from "@/src/commands/init"
8	import { mcp } from "@/src/commands/mcp"
9	import { migrate } from "@/src/commands/migrate"
10	import { registry } from "@/src/commands/registry"
11	import { search } from "@/src/commands/search"
12	import { view } from "@/src/commands/view"
13	import { Command } from "commander"
14	
15	import packageJson from "../package.json"
16	
17	process.on("SIGINT", () => process.exit(0))
18	process.on("SIGTERM", () => process.exit(0))
19	
20	async function main() {
21	  const program = new Command()
22	    .name("shadcn")
23	    .description("build your component library")
24	    .version(
25	      packageJson.version || "1.0.0",
26	      "-v, --version",
27	      "display the version number"
28	    )
29	
30	  program
31	    .addCommand(init)
32	    .addCommand(add)
33	    .addCommand(diff)
34	    .addCommand(docs)
35	    .addCommand(view)
36	    .addCommand(search)
37	    .addCommand(migrate)
38	    .addCommand(info)
39	    .addCommand(build)
40	    .addCommand(mcp)
41	    .addCommand(registry)
42	
43	  program.parse()
44	}
45	
46	main()
47	
48	export * from "./registry/api"
49

37	export function resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
38	  items: string[],
39	  config: Config
40	) {
41	  const registryHeaders: Record<string, Record<string, string>> = {}
42	  const resolvedItems = [...items]
43	
44	  if (!config?.registries) {
45	    setRegistryHeaders({})
46	    return resolvedItems
47	  }
48	
49	  for (let i = 0; i < resolvedItems.length; i++) {
50	    const resolved = buildUrlAndHeadersForRegistryItem(resolvedItems[i], config)
51	
52	    if (resolved) {
53	      resolvedItems[i] = resolved.url
54	
55	      if (Object.keys(resolved.headers).length > 0) {
56	        registryHeaders[resolved.url] = resolved.headers
57	      }
58	    }
59	  }
60	
61	  setRegistryHeaders(registryHeaders)
62	
63	  return resolvedItems
64	}
65	
66	// Internal function that fetches registry items without clearing context.
67	// This is used for recursive dependency resolution.
68	export async function fetchRegistryItems(
69	  items: string[],
70	  config: Config,
71	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
72	) {
73	  const results = await Promise.all(
74	    items.map(async (item) => {
75	      if (isLocalFile(item)) {
76	        return fetchRegistryLocal(item)
77	      }
78	
79	      if (isUrl(item)) {
80	        const [result] = await fetchRegistry([item], options)
81	        try {
82	          return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
83	        } catch (error) {
84	          throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
85	        }
86	      }
87	
88	      if (item.startsWith("@") && config?.registries) {
89	        const paths = resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries([item], config)
90	        const [result] = await fetchRegistry(paths, options)
91	        try {
92	          return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
93	        } catch (error) {
94	          throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
95	        }
96	      }
97	
98	      const path = `styles/${config?.style ?? "new-york-v4"}/${item}.json`
99	      const [result] = await fetchRegistry([path], options)
100	      try {
101	        return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
102	      } catch (error) {
103	        throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
104	      }
105	    })
106	  )
107	
108	  return results
109	}
110	
111	// Helper schema for items with source tracking.
112	const registryItemWithSourceSchema = registryItemCommonSchema
113	  .extend({
114	    type: registryItemTypeSchema,
115	    _source: z.string().optional(),
116	    // Optional fields for specific item types.
117	    font: registryItemFontSchema.optional(),
118	    config: z.any().optional(),
119	  })
120	  .passthrough()

122	// Resolves a list of registry items with all their dependencies and returns
123	// a complete installation bundle with merged configuration.
124	export async function resolveRegistryTree(
125	  names: z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>["name"][],
126	  config: Config,
127	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
128	) {
129	  options = {
130	    useCache: true,
131	    ...options,
132	  }
133	
134	  let payload: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
135	  let allDependencyItems: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
136	  let allDependencyRegistryNames: string[] = []
137	
138	  const uniqueNames = Array.from(new Set(names))
139	
140	  const results = await fetchRegistryItems(uniqueNames, config, options)
141	
142	  const resultMap = new Map<string, z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>>()
143	  for (let i = 0; i < results.length; i++) {
144	    if (results[i]) {
145	      resultMap.set(uniqueNames[i], results[i])
146	    }
147	  }
148	
149	  for (const [sourceName, item] of Array.from(resultMap.entries())) {
150	    // Add source tracking
151	    const itemWithSource: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema> = {
152	      ...item,
153	      _source: sourceName,
154	    }
155	    payload.push(itemWithSource)
156	
157	    if (item.registryDependencies) {
158	      // Resolve namespace syntax and set headers for dependencies
159	      let resolvedDependencies = item.registryDependencies
160	
161	      // Check for namespaced dependencies when no registries are configured
162	      if (!config?.registries) {
163	        const namespacedDeps = item.registryDependencies.filter((dep: string) =>
164	          dep.startsWith("@")
165	        )
166	        if (namespacedDeps.length > 0) {
167	          const { registry } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(namespacedDeps[0])
168	          throw new RegistryNotConfiguredError(registry)
169	        }
170	      } else {
171	        resolvedDependencies = resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
172	          item.registryDependencies,
173	          config
174	        )
175	      }
176	
177	      const { items, registryNames } = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
178	        resolvedDependencies,
179	        config,
180	        options,
181	        new Set(uniqueNames)
182	      )
183	      allDependencyItems.push(...items)
184	      allDependencyRegistryNames.push(...registryNames)
185	    }
186	  }
187	
188	  payload.push(...allDependencyItems)
189	
190	  // Handle any remaining registry names that need index resolution
191	  if (allDependencyRegistryNames.length > 0) {
192	    // Remove duplicates from registry names
193	    const uniqueRegistryNames = Array.from(new Set(allDependencyRegistryNames))
194	
195	    // Separate namespaced and non-namespaced items
196	    const nonNamespacedItems = uniqueRegistryNames.filter(
197	      (name) => !name.startsWith("@")
198	    )
199	    const namespacedDepItems = uniqueRegistryNames.filter((name) =>
200	      name.startsWith("@")
201	    )
202	
203	    // Handle namespaced dependency items
204	    if (namespacedDepItems.length > 0) {
205	      // This will now throw specific errors on failure
206	      const depResults = await fetchRegistryItems(
207	        namespacedDepItems,
208	        config,
209	        options
210	      )
211	
212	      for (let i = 0; i < depResults.length; i++) {
213	        const item = depResults[i]
214	        const itemWithSource: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema> = {
215	          ...item,
216	          _source: namespacedDepItems[i],
217	        }
218	        payload.push(itemWithSource)
219	      }
220	    }
221	
222	    // For non-namespaced items, we need the index and style resolution
223	    if (nonNamespacedItems.length > 0) {
224	      const index = await getShadcnRegistryIndex()
225	      if (!index && payload.length === 0) {
226	        return null
227	      }
228	
229	      if (index) {
230	        // If we're resolving the index, we want it to go first
231	        if (nonNamespacedItems.includes("index")) {
232	          nonNamespacedItems.unshift("index")
233	        }
234	
235	        // Resolve non-namespaced items through the existing flow
236	        // Get URLs for all registry items including their dependencies
237	        const registryUrls: string[] = []
238	        for (const name of nonNamespacedItems) {
239	          const itemDependencies = await resolveRegistryDependencies(
240	            name,
241	            config,
242	            options
243	          )
244	          registryUrls.push(...itemDependencies)
245	        }
246	
247	        // Deduplicate URLs
248	        const uniqueUrls = Array.from(new Set(registryUrls))
249	        let result = await fetchRegistry(uniqueUrls, options)
250	        const registryPayload = z.array(registryItemSchema).parse(result)
251	        payload.push(...registryPayload)
252	      }
253	    }
254	  }
255	
256	  if (!payload.length) {
257	    return null
258	  }
259	
260	  // No deduplication - we want to support multiple items with the same name from different sources
261	
262	  // If we're resolving the index, we want to fetch
263	  // the theme item if a base color is provided.
264	  // We do this for index only.
265	  // Other components will ship with their theme tokens.
266	  if (
267	    uniqueNames.includes("index") ||
268	    allDependencyRegistryNames.includes("index")
269	  ) {
270	    if (config.tailwind.baseColor) {
271	      const theme = await registryGetTheme(config.tailwind.baseColor, config)
272	      if (theme) {
273	        payload.unshift(theme)
274	      }
275	    }
276	  }
277	
278	  // Build source map for topological sort.
279	  const sourceMap = new Map<
280	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>,
281	    string
282	  >()
283	  payload.forEach((item) => {
284	    // Use the _source property if it was added, otherwise use the name.
285	    const source = item._source || item.name
286	    sourceMap.set(item, source)
287	  })
288	
289	  // Apply topological sort to ensure dependencies come before dependents.
290	  payload = topologicalSortRegistryItems(payload, sourceMap)
291	
292	  // Sort the payload so that registry:theme items come first,
293	  // while maintaining the relative order of all items.
294	  payload.sort((a, b) => {
295	    if (a.type === "registry:theme" && b.type !== "registry:theme") {
296	      return -1
297	    }
298	    if (a.type !== "registry:theme" && b.type === "registry:theme") {
299	      return 1
300	    }
301	    return 0
302	  })
303	
304	  let tailwind = {}
305	  payload.forEach((item) => {
306	    tailwind = deepmerge(tailwind, item.tailwind ?? {})
307	  })
308	
309	  let cssVars = {}
310	  payload.forEach((item) => {
311	    cssVars = deepmerge(cssVars, item.cssVars ?? {})
312	  })
313	
314	  let css = {}
315	  payload.forEach((item) => {
316	    css = deepmerge(css, item.css ?? {})
317	  })
318	
319	  let docs = ""
320	  payload.forEach((item) => {
321	    if (item.docs) {
322	      docs += `${item.docs}\n`
323	    }
324	  })
325	
326	  let envVars = {}
327	  payload.forEach((item) => {
328	    envVars = deepmerge(envVars, item.envVars ?? {})
329	  })
330	
331	  // Deduplicate files based on resolved target paths.
332	  const deduplicatedFiles = await deduplicateFilesByTarget(
333	    payload.map((item) => item.files ?? []),
334	    config
335	  )
336	
337	  // Collect font items.
338	  const fonts: RegistryFontItem[] = payload
339	    .filter((item) => item.type === "registry:font" && item.font)
340	    .map((item) => ({
341	      ...item,
342	      type: "registry:font" as const,
343	      font: item.font!,
344	    }))
345	
346	  const parsed = registryResolvedItemsTreeSchema.parse({
347	    dependencies: deepmerge.all(payload.map((item) => item.dependencies ?? [])),
348	    devDependencies: deepmerge.all(
349	      payload.map((item) => item.devDependencies ?? [])
350	    ),
351	    files: deduplicatedFiles,
352	    tailwind,
353	    cssVars,
354	    css,
355	    docs,
356	    fonts: fonts.length > 0 ? fonts : undefined,
357	  })
358	
359	  if (Object.keys(envVars).length > 0) {
360	    parsed.envVars = envVars
361	  }
362	
363	  return parsed
364	}
365	
366	async function resolveDependenciesRecursively(
367	  dependencies: string[],
368	  config: Config,
369	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {},
370	  visited: Set<string> = new Set()
371	) {
372	  const items: z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>[] = []
373	  const registryNames: string[] = []
374	
375	  for (const dep of dependencies) {
376	    if (visited.has(dep)) {
377	      continue
378	    }
379	    visited.add(dep)
380	
381	    // Handle URLs and local files directly.
382	    if (isUrl(dep) || isLocalFile(dep)) {
383	      const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
384	      if (item) {
385	        items.push(item)
386	        if (item.registryDependencies) {
387	          // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
388	          const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
389	            ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
390	                item.registryDependencies,
391	                config
392	              )
393	            : item.registryDependencies
394	
395	          const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
396	            resolvedDeps,
397	            config,
398	            options,
399	            visited
400	          )
401	          items.push(...nested.items)
402	          registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
403	        }
404	      }
405	    }
406	    // Handle namespaced items (e.g., @one/foo, @two/bar).
407	    else if (dep.startsWith("@") && config?.registries) {
408	      // Check if the registry exists.
409	      const { registry } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(dep)
410	      if (registry && !(registry in config.registries)) {
411	        throw new RegistryNotConfiguredError(registry)
412	      }
413	
414	      // Let getRegistryItem handle the namespaced item with config
415	      // This ensures proper authentication headers are used
416	      const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
417	      if (item) {
418	        items.push(item)
419	        if (item.registryDependencies) {
420	          // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
421	          const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
422	            ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
423	                item.registryDependencies,
424	                config
425	              )
426	            : item.registryDependencies
427	
428	          const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
429	            resolvedDeps,
430	            config,
431	            options,
432	            visited
433	          )
434	          items.push(...nested.items)
435	          registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
436	        }
437	      }
438	    }
439	    // Handle regular component names.
440	    else {
441	      registryNames.push(dep)
442	
443	      if (config) {
444	        try {
445	          const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
446	          if (item && item.registryDependencies) {
447	            // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
448	            const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
449	              ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
450	                  item.registryDependencies,
451	                  config
452	                )
453	              : item.registryDependencies
454	
455	            const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
456	              resolvedDeps,
457	              config,
458	              options,
459	              visited
460	            )
461	            items.push(...nested.items)
462	            registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
463	          }
464	        } catch (error) {
465	          // If we can't fetch the registry item, that's okay - we'll still
466	          // include the name.
467	        }
468	      }
469	    }
470	  }
471	
472	  return { items, registryNames }
473	}
474	
475	async function resolveRegistryDependencies(
476	  url: string,
477	  config: Config,
478	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
479	) {
480	  if (isUrl(url)) {
481	    return [url]
482	  }
483	
484	  const { registryNames } = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
485	    [url],
486	    config,
487	    options,
488	    new Set()
489	  )
490	
491	  const style = config.resolvedPaths?.cwd
492	    ? await getTargetStyleFromConfig(config.resolvedPaths.cwd, config.style)
493	    : config.style
494	
495	  const urls = registryNames.map((name) =>
496	    resolveRegistryUrl(isUrl(name) ? name : `styles/${style}/${name}.json`)
497	  )
498	
499	  return Array.from(new Set(urls))
500	}

506	  ])

510	
511	  // TODO: Move this to the registry i.e registry:theme.

530	      theme: {},
531	      light: {
532	        radius: "0.5rem",
533	      },

535	    },
536	  } satisfies z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
537	
538	  if (config.tailwind.cssVariables) {

540	      ...theme.tailwind.config.theme.extend.colors,
541	      ...buildTailwindThemeColorsFromCssVars(baseColor.cssVars.dark ?? {}),
542	    }

554	        ...theme.cssVars.dark,

558	    if (tailwindVersion === "v4" && baseColor.cssVarsV4) {

577	
578	function computeItemHash(
579	  item: Pick<z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>, "name">,
580	  source?: string
581	) {
582	  const identifier = source || item.name
583	
584	  const hash = createHash("sha256")
585	    .update(identifier)
586	    .digest("hex")
587	    .substring(0, 8)
588	
589	  return `${item.name}::${hash}`
590	}
591	
592	function extractItemIdentifierFromDependency(dependency: string) {
593	  if (isUrl(dependency)) {
594	    const url = new URL(dependency)
595	    const pathname = url.pathname
596	    const match = pathname.match(/\/([^/]+)\.json$/)
597	    const name = match ? match[1] : path.basename(pathname, ".json")
598	
599	    return {
600	      name,
601	      hash: computeItemHash({ name }, dependency),
602	    }
603	  }
604	
605	  if (isLocalFile(dependency)) {
606	    const match = dependency.match(/\/([^/]+)\.json$/)
607	    const name = match ? match[1] : path.basename(dependency, ".json")
608	
609	    return {
610	      name,
611	      hash: computeItemHash({ name }, dependency),
612	    }
613	  }
614	
615	  const { item } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(dependency)
616	  return {
617	    name: item,
618	    hash: computeItemHash({ name: item }, dependency),
619	  }
620	}
621	
622	function topologicalSortRegistryItems(
623	  items: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[],
624	  sourceMap: Map<z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>, string>
625	) {
626	  const itemMap = new Map<
627	    string,
628	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>
629	  >()
630	  const hashToItem = new Map<
631	    string,
632	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>
633	  >()
634	  const inDegree = new Map<string, number>()
635	  const adjacencyList = new Map<string, string[]>()
636	
637	  items.forEach((item) => {
638	    const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
639	    const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
640	
641	    itemMap.set(hash, item)
642	    hashToItem.set(hash, item)
643	    inDegree.set(hash, 0)
644	    adjacencyList.set(hash, [])
645	  })
646	
647	  // Build a map of dependency to possible items.
648	  const depToHashes = new Map<string, string[]>()
649	  items.forEach((item) => {
650	    const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
651	    const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
652	
653	    if (!depToHashes.has(item.name)) {
654	      depToHashes.set(item.name, [])
655	    }
656	    depToHashes.get(item.name)!.push(hash)
657	
658	    if (source !== item.name) {
659	      if (!depToHashes.has(source)) {
660	        depToHashes.set(source, [])
661	      }
662	      depToHashes.get(source)!.push(hash)
663	    }
664	  })
665	
666	  items.forEach((item) => {
667	    const itemSource = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
668	    const itemHash = computeItemHash(item, itemSource)
669	
670	    if (item.registryDependencies) {
671	      item.registryDependencies.forEach((dep) => {
672	        let depHash: string | undefined
673	
674	        const exactMatches = depToHashes.get(dep) || []
675	        if (exactMatches.length === 1) {
676	          depHash = exactMatches[0]
677	        } else if (exactMatches.length > 1) {
678	          // Multiple matches - try to disambiguate.
679	          // For now, just use the first one and warn.
680	          depHash = exactMatches[0]
681	        } else {
682	          const { name } = extractItemIdentifierFromDependency(dep)
683	          const nameMatches = depToHashes.get(name) || []
684	          if (nameMatches.length > 0) {
685	            depHash = nameMatches[0]
686	          }
687	        }
688	
689	        if (depHash && itemMap.has(depHash)) {
690	          adjacencyList.get(depHash)!.push(itemHash)
691	          inDegree.set(itemHash, inDegree.get(itemHash)! + 1)
692	        }
693	      })
694	    }
695	  })
696	
697	  // Implements Kahn's algorithm.
698	  const queue: string[] = []
699	  const sorted: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
700	
701	  inDegree.forEach((degree, hash) => {
702	    if (degree === 0) {
703	      queue.push(hash)
704	    }
705	  })
706	
707	  while (queue.length > 0) {
708	    const currentHash = queue.shift()!
709	    const item = itemMap.get(currentHash)!
710	    sorted.push(item)
711	
712	    adjacencyList.get(currentHash)!.forEach((dependentHash) => {
713	      const newDegree = inDegree.get(dependentHash)! - 1
714	      inDegree.set(dependentHash, newDegree)
715	
716	      if (newDegree === 0) {
717	        queue.push(dependentHash)
718	      }
719	    })
720	  }
721	
722	  if (sorted.length !== items.length) {
723	    // console.warn("Circular dependency detected in registry items")
724	    // Return all items even if there are circular dependencies
725	    // Items not in sorted are part of circular dependencies
726	    const sortedHashes = new Set(
727	      sorted.map((item) => {
728	        const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
729	        return computeItemHash(item, source)
730	      })
731	    )
732	
733	    items.forEach((item) => {
734	      const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
735	      const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
736	      if (!sortedHashes.has(hash)) {
737	        sorted.push(item)
738	      }
739	    })
740	  }
741	
742	  return sorted
743	}
744

1	# shadcn
2	
3	A CLI for adding components to your project.
4	
5	## create
6	
7	Use the `create` command to create a new project. You will be taken to a website to build your custom design system and choose your framework.
8	
9	```bash
10	npx shadcn create
11	```
12	
13	## init
14	
15	Use the `init` command to initialize dependencies for a new project.
16	
17	The `init` command installs dependencies, adds the `cn` util, configures Tailwind CSS, and CSS variables for the project.
18	
19	```bash
20	npx shadcn init
21	```
22	
23	## add
24	
25	Use the `add` command to add components to your project.
26	
27	The `add` command adds a component to your project and installs all required dependencies.
28	
29	```bash
30	npx shadcn add [component]
31	```
32	
33	### Example
34	
35	```bash
36	npx shadcn add alert-dialog
37	```
38	
39	You can also run the command without any arguments to view a list of all available components:
40	
41	```bash
42	npx shadcn add
43	```
44	
45	## Documentation
46	
47	Visit https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/cli to view the documentation.
48	
49	## License
50	
51	Licensed under the [MIT license](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/LICENSE.md).
52

1	# Contributing
2	
3	Thanks for your interest in contributing to ui.shadcn.com. We're happy to have you here.
4	
5	Please take a moment to review this document before submitting your first pull request. We also strongly recommend that you check for open issues and pull requests to see if someone else is working on something similar.
6	
7	If you need any help, feel free to reach out to [@shadcn](https://twitter.com/shadcn).
8	
9	## About this repository
10	
11	This repository is a monorepo.
12	
13	- We use [pnpm](https://pnpm.io) and [`workspaces`](https://pnpm.io/workspaces) for development.
14	- We use [Turborepo](https://turbo.build/repo) as our build system.
15	- We use [changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for managing releases.
16	
17	## Structure
18	
19	This repository is structured as follows:
20	
21	```
22	apps
23	└── v4
24	    ├── app
25	    ├── components
26	    ├── content
27	    └── registry
28	        └── new-york-v4
29	            ├── example
30	            └── ui
31	packages
32	└── shadcn
33	```
34	
35	| Path                 | Description                              |
36	| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
37	| `apps/v4/app`        | The Next.js application for the website. |
38	| `apps/v4/components` | The React components for the website.    |
39	| `apps/v4/content`    | The content for the website.             |
40	| `apps/v4/registry`   | The registry for the components.         |
41	| `packages/shadcn`    | The `shadcn` package.                    |
42	
43	## Development
44	
45	### Fork this repo
46	
47	You can fork this repo by clicking the fork button in the top right corner of this page.
48	
49	### Clone on your local machine
50	
51	```bash
52	git clone https://github.com/your-username/ui.git
53	```
54	
55	### Navigate to project directory
56	
57	```bash
58	cd ui
59	```
60	
61	### Create a new Branch
62	
63	```bash
64	git checkout -b my-new-branch
65	```
66	
67	### Install dependencies
68	
69	```bash
70	pnpm install
71	```
72	
73	### Run a workspace
74	
75	You can use the `pnpm --filter=[WORKSPACE]` command to start the development process for a workspace.
76	
77	#### Examples
78	
79	1. To run the `ui.shadcn.com` website:
80	
81	```bash
82	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
83	```
84	
85	2. To run the `shadcn` package:
86	
87	```bash
88	pnpm --filter=shadcn dev
89	```
90	
91	## Running the CLI Locally
92	
93	To run the CLI locally, you can follow the workflow:
94	
95	1. Start by running the dev server:
96	
97	   ```bash
98	   pnpm dev
99	   ```
100	
101	2. In another terminal tab, test the CLI by running:
102	
103	   ```bash
104	   pnpm shadcn
105	   ```
106	
107	   To test the CLI in a specific app, use a command like:
108	
109	   ```bash
110	   pnpm shadcn <init | add | ...> -c ~/Desktop/my-app
111	   ```
112	
113	This workflow ensures that you are running the most recent version of the registry and testing the CLI properly in your local environment.
114	
115	## Documentation
116	
117	The documentation for this project is located in the `v4` workspace. You can run the documentation locally by running the following command:
118	
119	```bash
120	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
121	```
122	
123	Documentation is written using [MDX](https://mdxjs.com). You can find the documentation files in the `apps/v4/content/docs` directory.
124	
125	## Components
126	
127	We use a registry system for developing components. You can find the source code for the components under `apps/v4/registry`. The components are organized by styles.
128	
129	```bash
130	apps
131	└── v4
132	    └── registry
133	        └── new-york-v4
134	            ├── example
135	            └── ui
136	```
137	
138	When adding or modifying components, please ensure that:
139	
140	1. You make the changes for every style.
141	2. You update the documentation.
142	3. You run `pnpm registry:build` to update the registry.
143	
144	## Commit Convention
145	
146	Before you create a Pull Request, please check whether your commits comply with
147	the commit conventions used in this repository.
148	
149	When you create a commit we kindly ask you to follow the convention
150	`category(scope or module): message` in your commit message while using one of
151	the following categories:
152	
153	- `feat / feature`: all changes that introduce completely new code or new
154	  features
155	- `fix`: changes that fix a bug (ideally you will additionally reference an
156	  issue if present)
157	- `refactor`: any code related change that is not a fix nor a feature
158	- `docs`: changing existing or creating new documentation (i.e. README, docs for
159	  usage of a lib or cli usage)
160	- `build`: all changes regarding the build of the software, changes to
161	  dependencies or the addition of new dependencies
162	- `test`: all changes regarding tests (adding new tests or changing existing
163	  ones)
164	- `ci`: all changes regarding the configuration of continuous integration (i.e.
165	  github actions, ci system)
166	- `chore`: all changes to the repository that do not fit into any of the above
167	  categories
168	
169	  e.g. `feat(components): add new prop to the avatar component`
170	
171	If you are interested in the detailed specification you can visit
172	https://www.conventionalcommits.org/ or check out the
173	[Angular Commit Message Guidelines](https://github.com/angular/angular/blob/22b96b9/CONTRIBUTING.md#-commit-message-guidelines).
174	
175	## Requests for new components
176	
177	If you have a request for a new component, please open a discussion on GitHub. We'll be happy to help you out.
178	
179	## CLI
180	
181	The `shadcn` package is a CLI for adding components to your project. You can find the documentation for the CLI [here](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/cli).
182	
183	Any changes to the CLI should be made in the `packages/shadcn` directory. If you can, it would be great if you could add tests for your changes.
184	
185	## Testing
186	
187	Tests are written using [Vitest](https://vitest.dev). You can run all the tests from the root of the repository.
188	
189	```bash
190	pnpm test
191	```
192	
193	Please ensure that the tests are passing when submitting a pull request. If you're adding new features, please include tests.
194

41	function Button({
42	  className,
43	  variant = "default",
44	  size = "default",
45	  asChild = false,
46	  ...props
47	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
48	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
49	    asChild?: boolean
50	  }) {
51	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
52	
53	  return (
54	    <Comp
55	      data-slot="button"
56	      data-variant={variant}
57	      data-size={size}
58	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the architecture. Let me compile my findings:

## shadcn/ui Component Library - Architecture Overview

Based on my exploration of the repository, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the overall structure and how the component registry system works:

### 1. **Root Directory Structure**

```
/
├── apps/                       # Application workspaces
│   └── v4/                    # Main website & component registry
├── packages/                   # Package workspaces
│   ├── shadcn/                # CLI tool (main package)
│   └── tests/                 # Test suite
├── templates/                  # Project templates (Next, Vite, React Router, etc.)
├── skills/                     # AI/CLI skills
├── scripts/                    # Build and utility scripts
├── CONTRIBUTING.md            # Contribution guidelines
├── package.json              # Root workspace config
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml       # pnpm workspace definition
├── turbo.json                # Turborepo configuration
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript config
├── vitest.config.ts          # Test configuration
└── .changeset/               # Changesets for versioning
```

### 2. **Main Registry System Files**

The registry is implemented in `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/`:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **api.ts** | Main API exports for fetching registries, items, and resolving dependencies |
| **schema.ts** | Zod validation schemas for all registry types (registry items, configs, presets) |
| **resolver.ts** | Resolves component trees with full dependency resolution using topological sorting |
| **builder.ts** | Builds URLs and headers for registry items based on config |
| **fetcher.ts** | Fetches registry data from URLs or local files |
| **parser.ts** | Parses namespaced registry item strings (e.g., `@shadcn/button`) |
| **constants.ts** | Registry constants and built-in registries |
| **config.ts** | Configuration handling |
| **index.ts** | Public exports |

### 3. **Component Storage & Organization**

Components are stored in `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/`:

```
new-york-v4/
├── ui/                    # Core UI components
│   ├── button.tsx         # Individual component files
│   ├── accordion.tsx
│   ├── _registry.ts       # Component registry definition (all UI components)
│   └── ... (59+ components)
├── blocks/                # Pre-built component blocks
├── charts/                # Chart components
├── examples/              # Component examples/demos
├── hooks/                 # Custom React hooks
├── lib/                   # Utility libraries
├── internal/              # Internal utilities
└── registry.ts            # Main registry aggregator
```

### 4. **Registry System Architecture**

The registry uses a **hierarchical, schema-based system** with these key concepts:

#### **Registry Item Schema** (defined in `schema.ts`):
- Each component is a registry item with metadata:
  ```typescript
  {
    name: "alert-dialog",
    type: "registry:ui",                    // Type categories: ui, hook, block, etc.
    dependencies: ["radix-ui"],              // npm dependencies
    registryDependencies: ["button"],        // Other components it depends on
    files: [
      {
        path: "ui/alert-dialog.tsx",
        type: "registry:ui",
        target: "ui/alert-dialog.tsx"       // Where to install
      }
    ],
    tailwind?: { config: {} },              // Tailwind config merging
    cssVars?: { theme: {}, light: {}, dark: {} },  // CSS variables
    css?: {},                               // Global CSS
    envVars?: {}                            // Environment variables
  }
  ```

#### **Registry Types** (from `schema.ts` enum):
- `registry:ui` - UI components
- `registry:hook` - React hooks
- `registry:lib` - Utility libraries
- `registry:block` - Pre-built blocks
- `registry:component` - Components
- `registry:page` - Full pages
- `registry:file` - Generic files
- `registry:theme` - Theme configurations
- `registry:style` - Style definitions
- `registry:base` - Base configurations
- `registry:font` - Font definitions
- `registry:item` - Generic items

### 5. **How the Registry System Works**

**Fetching & Resolving Flow:**

1. **Registry Config**: Users can configure custom registries in `components.json`
   ```json
   {
     "registries": {
       "@acme": "https://registry.example.com/{name}.json",
       "@v0": {
         "url": "https://v0.dev/api/{name}",
         "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ${AUTH_TOKEN}" },
         "params": { "api_key": "${API_KEY}" }
       }
     }
   }
   ```

2. **Built-in Registry**: Default is `@shadcn` mapped to `https://ui.shadcn.com/r/styles/{style}/{name}.json`

3. **Dependency Resolution** (in `resolver.ts`):
   - Uses topological sorting (Kahn's algorithm) to order dependencies
   - Resolves both `dependencies` (npm) and `registryDependencies` (other components)
   - Supports namespaced items (e.g., `@shadcn/button`, `@acme/custom-component`)
   - Handles circular dependency detection
   - Merges Tailwind config, CSS variables, and other metadata

4. **Item Registration** (in `_registry.ts` files):
   - Each style (new-york-v4) has its own registry definition
   - Components are defined as arrays in `_registry.ts` files
   - Registry aggregates all items: UI, blocks, charts, hooks, themes, examples

### 6. **Demo/Example Files Location**

Examples are stored in `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/`:
- Each component has example implementations
- Examples are part of the registry with type `registry:example`
- Used to display working component demos on the website

### 7. **Configuration Files**

**Key Config Files:**
- **`components.json`** (in `/apps/v4/`): Defines paths, Tailwind config, aliases
- **`package.json`** (shadcn): Exports the CLI tool and registry module
- **`tsconfig.json`**: Path aliases (`@/` points to project root)
- **`.prettierrc`**, **`.eslintrc.json`**: Code style enforcement

### 8. **How to Add New Components**

Based on CONTRIBUTING.md and code patterns:

1. **Create component file**: Add `.tsx` file to `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`
2. **Add to registry**: Update `ui/_registry.ts` with component metadata:
   ```typescript
   {
     name: "my-component",
     type: "registry:ui",
     dependencies: ["required-deps"],
     registryDependencies: ["other-components"],
     files: [{
       path: "ui/my-component.tsx",
       type: "registry:ui"
     }]
   }
   ```
3. **Replicate for all styles**: Must update for every style variant
4. **Update documentation**: Add docs in `/apps/v4/content/docs`
5. **Build registry**: Run `pnpm registry:build`

### 9. **Key Features of the Registry**

- **Multi-style support**: Different visual styles (new-york-v4) with same component names
- **Authentication**: Supports custom headers with auth tokens
- **Environment variables**: Config can use `${ENV_VAR}` syntax
- **Query parameters**: Registry URLs support custom query params
- **CSS Variable theming**: Components support theme customization
- **Tailwind merging**: Configurations automatically merged using `deepmerge`
- **Namespacing**: Support for custom registries with `@namespace` prefix
- **Version management**: Uses changesets for release management

### 10. **Commands Available** (in `/packages/shadcn/src/commands/`)

- `init` - Initialize project
- `add` - Add components to project
- `diff` - Show differences
- `build` - Build registry
- `search` - Search components
- `view` - View component details
- `docs` - Open documentation
- `info` - Show project info
- `registry` - Registry commands
- `mcp` - Model Context Protocol support
- `migrate` - Migration utilities

This architecture allows shadcn/ui to be both a pre-built component library AND a framework for building custom component libraries, with powerful dependency management and multi-variant support.

├── package.json              # Root workspace config
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml       # pnpm workspace definition
├── turbo.json                # Turborepo configuration
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript config
├── vitest.config.ts          # Test configuration
└── .changeset/               # Changesets for versioning
```

### 2. **Main Registry System Files**

| **schema.ts** | Zod validation schemas for all registry types (registry items, configs, presets) |
| **resolver.ts** | Resolves component trees with full dependency resolution using topological sorting |
| **builder.ts** | Builds URLs and headers for registry items based on config |
| **fetcher.ts** | Fetches registry data from URLs or local files |
| **parser.ts** | Parses namespaced registry item strings (e.g., `@shadcn/button`) |
| **constants.ts** | Registry constants and built-in registries |
| **config.ts** | Configuration handling |
| **index.ts** | Public exports |

new-york-v4/
├── ui/                    # Core UI components
│   ├── button.tsx         # Individual component files
│   ├── accordion.tsx

├── blocks/                # Pre-built component blocks

  }

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the shadcn/ui patterns. Let me create a detailed analysis report for you.

## Comprehensive Analysis: shadcn/ui Compound Component Patterns

Based on my exploration of the new-york-v4 registry, here's the complete breakdown of how compound components are structured in this library:

---

## 1. **Compound Component TypeScript Patterns**

### **Core Principles:**

All compound components follow these consistent patterns:

**Wrapper Components (compose existing primitives):**
- Use `"use client"` at the top for client-side functionality
- Import primitive components from `radix-ui`
- Wrap primitives with minimal abstraction
- Export multiple sub-components designed to work together

**Key Characteristics:**

1. **"use client" directive** - Required for interactive components
2. **Imports from radix-ui** - Base primitives from the underlying library
3. **CN utility** - For conditional class merging (`import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"`)
4. **Data-slot attributes** - Semantic naming for CSS selectors (e.g., `data-slot="accordion"`, `data-slot="accordion-item"`)
5. **Function composition** - Each sub-component is a named function exported separately

### **File: accordion.tsx** (Lines 1-66)
- **Root wrapper:** `Accordion` component wraps `AccordionPrimitive.Root`
- **Item sub-component:** `AccordionItem` wraps `AccordionPrimitive.Item`
- **Trigger sub-component:** `AccordionTrigger` wraps `AccordionPrimitive.Trigger` with icon
- **Content sub-component:** `AccordionContent` wraps `AccordionPrimitive.Content`
- **Pattern:** Uses `data-slot` attributes for CSS targeting
- **Icons:** Uses `lucide-react` for ChevronDownIcon with rotation on state change
- **CSS:** Extensive Tailwind classes with data-state selectors (e.g., `[&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180`)

### **File: tabs.tsx** (Lines 1-91)
- **Root wrapper:** `Tabs` component with orientation support
- **List sub-component:** `TabsList` uses CVA variants ("default" or "line")
- **Trigger sub-component:** `TabsTrigger` with complex styling for different orientations
- **Content sub-component:** `TabsContent` wrapper
- **Pattern:** Uses `cva` (class-variance-authority) for variant management
- **Variant structure:** Passes variant prop through data attributes
- **Group selectors:** Uses group-data CSS utilities for parent-child styling
- **Export:** Exports both component and `tabsListVariants` CVA object

### **File: dialog.tsx** (Lines 1-158)
- **Multiple sub-components:** Root, Trigger, Portal, Close, Overlay, Content, Header, Footer, Title, Description
- **Composition pattern:** Some components are purely wrapper functions, others add substantial UI (DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogFooter)
- **Conditional props:** DialogContent accepts custom `showCloseButton` prop
- **Nested composition:** DialogContent internally composes DialogPortal and DialogOverlay
- **Helper divs:** DialogHeader and DialogFooter are semantic wrapper divs with Tailwind classes

### **File: breadcrumb.tsx** (Lines 1-109)
- **Semantic HTML:** Uses nav, ol, li, a, span elements
- **asChild pattern:** Supports `asChild` prop with `Slot.Root` from radix-ui for polymorphism
- **Icon integration:** Default separator icon (ChevronRight) with customizable children
- **SR labels:** Uses aria-hidden and role attributes for accessibility
- **Semantic slots:** BreadcrumbPage uses aria-current="page"

### **File: card.tsx** (Lines 1-92)
- **Simple div wrappers:** No Radix primitive usage
- **Container pattern:** Card is the root layout component
- **Sub-components:** Header, Title, Description, Action, Content, Footer
- **Semantic sections:** Each has specific Tailwind classes for layout (px-6 padding, gap-6)
- **Container queries:** CardHeader uses @container for responsive behavior

---

## 2. **Registry Entry Patterns**

### **UI Component Registry** (`_registry.ts`)

**Accordion entry (lines 4-14):**
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Key fields:**
- `name` - Kebab-case component name
- `type: "registry:ui"` - Identifies this as a UI component
- `dependencies` - External npm packages (not shadcn components)
- `registryDependencies` - References to other shadcn components
- `files` - Array of file objects with path and type

**Tabs entry (lines 586-595):**
```typescript
{
  name: "tabs",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/tabs.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Complex example - sidebar (lines 460-520):**
```typescript
{
  name: "sidebar",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui", "class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  registryDependencies: [
    "button",
    "separator",
    "sheet",
    "tooltip",
    "input",
    "use-mobile",
    "skeleton",
  ],
  files: [{path: "ui/sidebar.tsx", type: "registry:ui"}],
  tailwind: {
    config: {
      theme: {
        extend: {
          colors: {
            sidebar: {
              DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--sidebar-background))",
              // ... CSS variable definitions
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
  cssVars: {
    light: {"sidebar-background": "0 0% 98%", ...},
    dark: {"sidebar-background": "240 5.9% 10%", ...},
  },
}
```

**Registry fields breakdown:**
- `dependencies` - npm packages required
- `registryDependencies` - other shadcn components the component imports
- `files` - each component file with type
- `tailwind` (optional) - Tailwind config extensions
- `cssVars` (optional) - CSS variable definitions for light/dark themes
- `docs` (optional) - Usage documentation (seen in tooltip)

---

## 3. **Example Registry Patterns**

### **Example entries** (from examples/_registry.ts)

**Accordion demo (lines 4-14):**
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["accordion"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Tabs demo (lines 2148-2157):**
```typescript
{
  name: "tabs-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["tabs"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/tabs-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Key differences from UI registry:**
- `type: "registry:example"` instead of "registry:ui"
- `registryDependencies` points to UI component(s), not external dependencies
- Single file examples (no separate files array complexity)

---

## 4. **React Context & State Management**

**Finding:** The compound components in this codebase **do NOT use React Context** for state management. Instead, they:

1. **Rely on Radix UI primitives** for all state management
2. **Radix-ui handles:** Open/closed state, active states, controlled/uncontrolled modes
3. **Shadow DOM style:** Data attributes reflect state (`data-state=open`, `data-state=closed`, `data-state=active`)

**Example from accordion.tsx:**
- State is managed by `AccordionPrimitive.Root`
- Components respond to state via Tailwind selectors: `data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down`

**Example from tabs.tsx:**
- State managed by `TabsPrimitive.Root`
- Styling responds to `data-state=active` on TabsTrigger
- Orientation handled via `data-orientation` on root

---

## 5. **CSS/Tailwind Class Patterns**

### **Consistent patterns across all components:**

1. **Data-slot attributes** for identifying components:
   ```typescript
   data-slot="accordion"
   data-slot="accordion-item"
   data-slot="accordion-trigger"
   data-slot="accordion-content"
   ```

2. **Group selectors** for parent-child relationships:
   ```typescript
   // From tabs.tsx
   "group/tabs flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col"
   "group/tabs-list inline-flex..."
   "group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:data-[state=active]:shadow-sm"
   ```

3. **Data attribute selectors** for state:
   ```typescript
   "data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down"
   "data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up"
   "data-[state=active]:bg-background"
   ```

4. **SVG size utilities**:
   ```typescript
   "[&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4"
   ```

5. **Focus visible patterns**:
   ```typescript
   "focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50"
   ```

6. **Dark mode support**:
   ```typescript
   "dark:text-muted-foreground dark:hover:text-foreground"
   ```

---

## 6. **Component Sub-component Patterns**

### **Type A: Wrapper Components (no logic)**
- Just wrap a primitive and apply Tailwind classes
- Examples: AccordionItem, TabsContent, BreadcrumbItem
- 5-10 lines of code

### **Type B: Enhanced Wrappers (add props/features)**
- Wrap primitive but add custom props or conditional rendering
- Examples: DialogContent (has `showCloseButton`), TabsList (has `variant`)
- Use CVA for variant management (TabsList, Button)
- 10-30 lines of code

### **Type C: Semantic Wrappers (layout helpers)**
- No primitive involved, just HTML divs with Tailwind
- Examples: CardHeader, DialogHeader, BreadcrumbList
- 5-10 lines of code

### **Type D: Composition Components (complex assembly)**
- Internally compose other sub-components
- Example: DialogContent composes Portal + Overlay + Content + optional Close button
- 20-40 lines of code

---

## 7. **Import Patterns**

**Standard imports in all components:**
```typescript
import * as React from "react"           // React types
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"         // Class merging utility
import { [Primitive] from "radix-ui"     // Radix primitives
import { [Icon] } from "lucide-react"    // Icons (optional)
import { cva } from "class-variance-authority"  // Variants (optional)
import { Slot } from "radix-ui"          // For asChild pattern (optional)
```

---

## 8. **Separator Component** (`separator.tsx`)

Simple primitive wrapper - **7 lines of code**:
- Takes orientation prop (default "horizontal")
- Uses `data-slot="separator"`
- Responsive classes: `data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px`
- No sub-components
- No dependencies beyond radix-ui

**Registry entry (lines 438-447):**
```typescript
{
  name: "separator",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  files: [{path: "ui/separator.tsx", type: "registry:ui"}],
}
```

---

## 9. **No Stepper Component**

**Finding:** There is **NO existing stepper component** in this registry. This is a good candidate for a new compound component implementation following the patterns above.

---

## 10. **Key Takeaways for New Stepper Component**

Based on this analysis, here's how to structure a new Stepper component:

### **Component structure:**
```
stepper.tsx
├── Stepper (root) - wraps Radix or custom Root
├── StepperItem - individual step container
├── StepperTrigger/StepperHeader - step label/indicator
├── StepperContent - step content area
└── StepperDescription - step description text
```

### **Implementation approach:**
1. **Use Radix UI primitives** if available (check for @radix-ui/stepper)
2. **If no Radix primitive:** Create custom state management (likely context-based since Radix won't handle it)
3. **Data-slot attributes:** `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-slot="stepper-item"`, `data-slot="stepper-trigger"`, etc.
4. **State via data attributes:** `data-state=active/inactive`, `data-completed=true/false`, `data-index=0`
5. **CVA variants:** For step variant styles (dot, circle, number, line)
6. **Tailwind styling:** Extensive use of group selectors and data attributes
7. **Registry entry:** Add to _registry.ts with appropriate dependencies

### **Registry entry template:**
```typescript
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui", "class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  registryDependencies: ["separator"],  // Likely needs separator for lines
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

---

**File Paths Referenced:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474035126-4uvz06/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474035126-4uvz06/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474035126-4uvz06/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dialog.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474035126-4uvz06/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/breadcrumb.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474035126-4uvz06/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/card.tsx`

### **Example entries** (from examples/_registry.ts)

**Accordion demo (lines 4-14):**
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["accordion"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Tabs demo (lines 2148-2157):**
```typescript
{
  name: "tabs-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["tabs"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/tabs-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Key differences from UI registry:**
- `type: "registry:example"` instead of "registry:ui"
- `registryDependencies` points to UI component(s), not external dependencies
- Single file examples (no separate files array complexity)

---

## 4. **React Context & State Management**

**Finding:** The compound components in this codebase **do NOT use React Context** for state management. Instead, they:

1. **Rely on Radix UI primitives** for all state management
2. **Radix-ui handles:** Open/closed state, active states, controlled/uncontrolled modes
3. **Shadow DOM style:** Data attributes reflect state (`data-state=open`, `data-state=closed`, `data-state=active`)

**Example from accordion.tsx:**
- State is managed by `AccordionPrimitive.Root`
- Components respond to state via Tailwind selectors: `data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down`

**Example from tabs.tsx:**
- State managed by `TabsPrimitive.Root`
- Styling responds to `data-state=active` on TabsTrigger
- Orientation handled via `data-orientation` on root

---

## 5. **CSS/Tailwind Class Patterns**

### **Consistent patterns across all components:**

1. **Data-slot attributes** for identifying components:
   ```typescript
   data-slot="accordion"
   data-slot="accordion-item"
   data-slot="accordion-trigger"
   data-slot="accordion-content"
   ```

2. **Group selectors** for parent-child relationships:
   ```typescript
   // From tabs.tsx
   "group/tabs flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col"
   "group/tabs-list inline-flex..."
   "group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:data-[state=active]:shadow-sm"
   ```

3. **Data attribute selectors** for state:
   ```typescript
   "data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down"
   "data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up"
   "data-[state=active]:bg-background"
   ```

4. **SVG size utilities**:
   ```typescript
   "[&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4"
   ```

5. **Focus visible patterns**:
   ```typescript
   "focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50"
   ```

6. **Dark mode support**:
   ```typescript
   "dark:text-muted-foreground dark:hover:text-foreground"
   ```

---

## 6. **Component Sub-component Patterns**

### **Type A: Wrapper Components (no logic)**
- Just wrap a primitive and apply Tailwind classes
- Examples: AccordionItem, TabsContent, BreadcrumbItem
- 5-10 lines of code

### **Type B: Enhanced Wrappers (add props/features)**
- Wrap primitive but add custom props or conditional rendering
- Examples: DialogContent (has `showCloseButton`), TabsList (has `variant`)
- Use CVA for variant management (TabsList, Button)
- 10-30 lines of code

### **Type C: Semantic Wrappers (layout helpers)**
- No primitive involved, just HTML divs with Tailwind
- Examples: CardHeader, DialogHeader, BreadcrumbList
- 5-10 lines of code

### **Type D: Composition Components (complex assembly)**
- Internally compose other sub-components
- Example: DialogContent composes Portal + Overlay + Content + optional Close button
- 20-40 lines of code

---

## 7. **Import Patterns**

**Standard imports in all components:**
```typescript
import * as React from "react"           // React types
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"         // Class merging utility
import { [Primitive] from "radix-ui"     // Radix primitives
import { [Icon] } from "lucide-react"    // Icons (optional)
import { cva } from "class-variance-authority"  // Variants (optional)
import { Slot } from "radix-ui"          // For asChild pattern (optional)
```

---

## 8. **Separator Component** (`separator.tsx`)

Simple primitive wrapper - **7 lines of code**:
- Takes orientation prop (default "horizontal")
- Uses `data-slot="separator"`
- Responsive classes: `data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px`
- No sub-components
- No dependencies beyond radix-ui

**Registry entry (lines 438-447):**
```typescript
{
  name: "separator",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  files: [{path: "ui/separator.tsx", type: "registry:ui"}],
}
```

---

## 9. **No Stepper Component**

**Finding:** There is **NO existing stepper component** in this registry. This is a good candidate for a new compound component implementation following the patterns above.

---

## 10. **Key Takeaways for New Stepper Component**

Based on this analysis, here's how to structure a new Stepper component:

### **Component structure:**
```
stepper.tsx
├── Stepper (root) - wraps Radix or custom Root
├── StepperItem - individual step container
├── StepperTrigger/StepperHeader - step label/indicator
├── StepperContent - step content area
└── StepperDescription - step description text
```

### **Implementation approach:**
1. **Use Radix UI primitives** if available (check for @radix-ui/stepper)
2. **If no Radix primitive:** Create custom state management (likely context-based since Radix won't handle it)
3. **Data-slot attributes:** `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-slot="stepper-item"`, `data-slot="stepper-trigger"`, etc.
4. **State via data attributes:** `data-state=active/inactive`, `data-completed=true/false`, `data-index=0`
5. **CVA variants:** For step variant styles (dot, circle, number, line)
6. **Tailwind styling:** Extensive use of group selectors and data attributes
7. **Registry entry:** Add to _registry.ts with appropriate dependencies

### **Registry entry template:**
```typescript
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui", "class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  registryDependencies: ["separator"],  // Likely needs separator for lines
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

Key findings:
1. The stepper component should go alphabetically between "spinner" and "switch" in both registries
2. No Radix UI primitive exists for a stepper, so we need to use React context for state management
3. The component should follow all the patterns seen in accordion.tsx and tabs.tsx
4. Sub-components needed: Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperTitle, StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent


5. I need to support both horizontal and vertical orientations with proper data attributes like `data-slot`, `data-state`, and `data-orientation` to handle styling variations.

3	import * as React from "react"
4	import { Switch as SwitchPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
5	
6	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
7	
8	function Switch({
9	  className,
10	  size = "default",
11	  ...props
12	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof SwitchPrimitive.Root> & {
13	  size?: "sm" | "default"
14	}) {
15	  return (
16	    <SwitchPrimitive.Root
17	      data-slot="switch"
18	      data-size={size}
19	      className={cn(
20	        "peer group/switch inline-flex shrink-0 items-center rounded-full border border-transparent shadow-xs transition-all outline-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 data-[size=default]:h-[1.15rem] data-[size=default]:w-8 data-[size=sm]:h-3.5 data-[size=sm]:w-6 data-[state=checked]:bg-primary data-[state=unchecked]:bg-input dark:data-[state=unchecked]:bg-input/80",
21	        className
22	      )}
23	      {...props}
24	    >
25	      <SwitchPrimitive.Thumb
26	        data-slot="switch-thumb"
27	        className={cn(
28	          "pointer-events-none block rounded-full bg-background ring-0 transition-transform group-data-[size=default]/switch:size-4 group-data-[size=sm]/switch:size-3 data-[state=checked]:translate-x-[calc(100%-2px)] data-[state=unchecked]:translate-x-0 dark:data-[state=checked]:bg-primary-foreground dark:data-[state=unchecked]:bg-foreground"
29	        )}
30	      />
31	    </SwitchPrimitive.Root>
32	  )
33	}
34	
35	export { Switch }
36

content.thinking:
I have all the information I need. Now let me review the plan carefully and then write the final plan file. Let me also check a few things:

1. The `completed` prop logic - if `completed={false}` is passed but `value > step` is true, the step will still be shown as completed. This might be acceptable but I want to note it.

2. Let me look at the demo more carefully - in horizontal orientation, having `StepperContent` inside `StepperItem` makes each item contain its content, and a `flex-col` layout shows trigger+content stacked. This is fine for horizontal.

3. The separator in horizontal orientation needs to sit between items in the row. 

In horizontal mode, the stepper root uses `flex-row` so the separator sits as a horizontal line between items as they grow with `flex-1`. For vertical orientation, I need to apply styles that transform the separator into a vertical line with proper spacing and stretching.

But I'm reconsidering the horizontal layout—if the separator is just `h-px flex-1` between items in a flex row, it would be a thin line taking up space, which might not look right visually.

The real issue is that items are `flex-col` and centered, so their height comes from content, while the separator at `h-px` would be too small to align properly with the step indicators. I need to add vertical positioning like `self-center` or a top margin to make the separator sit at the right height relative to the step circles.

For horizontal steppers, I'm also noticing that having content appear directly below each step could create a fragmented layout—typically you'd want a single content area below all the steps instead. I see the potential issue with uneven heights in horizontal mode when content is nested inside items—only the active step's content displays, so that item would be taller than the others. The demo structure works reasonably well despite this, so I'll move forward with the plan. Now I'm checking the exact line numbers in the UI registry where I need to insert the stepper component. I'm checking whether the stepper-demo registry entry needs to list button as a dependency, since it imports Button in the component. Looking at other examples like tabs-demo, it only lists the primary component being demonstrated, but alert-dialog includes both alert-dialog and button in its registryDependencies, so there's some inconsistency in how dependencies are declared.

Given that stepper-demo imports Button directly, I should include both stepper and button in the registryDependencies to be safe. I'm also noting that StepperContent placement in horizontal mode might be a design consideration worth acknowledging in the plan, then I'll write out the final plan file.

content.text: I have everything I need. Let me now write the final plan file.

content.text:
Here's the full plan for the **Stepper** compound component. Everything has been researched and designed — no existing files will be touched except to add registry entries.

---

## Summary

### 4 files total

| Action | File |
|---|---|
| **Create** | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` |
| **Create** | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` |
| **Edit** (entry only) | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` |
| **Edit** (entry only) | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` |

### Component architecture

No Radix UI primitive exists for steppers, so the component uses **two-level React context**:
- **`StepperContext`** (root) — holds `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`
- **`StepperItemContext`** (per-item) — holds `step`, `isActive`, `isCompleted`

**7 exported sub-components**: `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent`

### Key design choices
- **Orientation** propagates via CSS `group-data-[orientation=...]/stepper:` selectors (no prop drilling) — identical to how `tabs.tsx` works
- **`StepperTrigger`** has a built-in circular indicator (number or ✓ checkmark) — mirrors how `AccordionTrigger` has a built-in chevron
- **`StepperContent`** shows/hides via `data-[state=inactive]:hidden` — same pattern as Accordion/Tabs
- **`isCompleted`** = `completed prop || value > step` — supports both explicit prop control and auto linear progression
- **Registry entries** inserted alphabetically between "spinner"→"switch" and "switch-demo"→"table-demo"
- **`lucide-react`** is the only dependency (for `CheckIcon`)

## ✅ Stepper Component — Done

### Files created

**`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`** (297 lines)  
A fully self-contained compound component built on two React contexts:

| Sub-component | Role |
|---|---|
| `Stepper` | Root container — owns `value`/`onValueChange`/`orientation` state via `StepperContext` |
| `StepperItem` | Per-step wrapper — provides `step`, `isActive`, `isCompleted` via `StepperItemContext` |
| `StepperTrigger` | Clickable `<button>` with a built-in circular indicator (number **or** ✓ checkmark when completed) |
| `StepperTitle` | Step title `<span>` — dims when inactive |
| `StepperDescription` | Small subtitle `<span>` in muted foreground |
| `StepperSeparator` | Connecting line between steps — turns `bg-primary` once the step is completed |
| `StepperContent` | Step body panel — hidden via `data-[state=inactive]:hidden` |

Key design choices from the plan:
- Orientation propagates through `data-orientation` → `group-data-[orientation=…]/stepper-item:` Tailwind selectors (no prop drilling)
- Supports **controlled** (`value` + `onValueChange`) and **uncontrolled** (`defaultValue`) modes
- `isCompleted = completedProp ?? (value > step)` — supports both auto-linear progression and explicit override
- Only dependency: `lucide-react` (for `CheckIcon`)

**`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`** (96 lines)  
Interactive demo showing both **horizontal** and **vertical** orientations driven by shared state, with Back/Next buttons.
